methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: X-Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (IPO), post-money $2B+

data.narrative:
Deep Fission, founded by Liz Muller and Rich Muller, is a nuclear energy company preparing for IPO after raising $80M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 total comparables: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including X-Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. While 8 same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-stage comparables constrain traditional multiples analysis—only 1 company matches Deep Fission's IPO stage, with 0 at adjacent stages and 0 at similar valuation tiers. A median derived from all-sector nuclear peers would risk obscuring meaningful stage and scale differences. This limitation reflects the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the frontier nuclear market, where company trajectories, technology maturity, and capital requirements vary substantially.

Without triangulation across multiple valuation methods, establishing a supported range remains premature. The analysis reveals a critical constraint: Deep Fission's IPO positioning and the sparse peer set preclude reliable derivation of a median-based valuation. Prospective methodologies—discounted cash flow modeling tied to project timelines, sum-of-the-parts analysis by reactor type or geography, or revenue multiple comparisons if near-term revenues are defined—would require additional data not present here. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking trajectory, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital efficiency relative to public peer benchmarks.
